section.name: restic-offsite
section.summary: Restic encrypted offsite backup setup: repository initialization, backup/forget/prune workflow, restore commands, and password management rules.

rule.statement: Store the restic repository password in a password manager and in a local ~/.restic-password file (chmod 600), not in the backup itself.
rule.rationale: If the repository password is stored inside the restic repository, it is inaccessible if you need to recover from a total server loss — which is the primary disaster scenario. The password must live somewhere independent of the backup.

rule.statement: Run restic forget with --prune in the same command as the backup job, not as a separate weekly task.
rule.rationale: Running forget without prune marks snapshots for deletion but does not reclaim space. Running prune separately doubles the number of cron jobs to maintain. Combined, retention enforcement is always current.

example.code:
# Install restic sudo apt install restic
# Set environment variables (from .env) export RESTIC_REPOSITORY="b2:nero-backups:server" export RESTIC_PASSWORD_FILE="/home/nero/.restic-password" export B2_ACCOUNT_ID="..." export B2_ACCOUNT_KEY="..."
# Initialize repository (one-time) restic init
# Verify repository health restic check

# Run backup restic backup \ /home/nero/backups/database/ \ /home/nero/backups/redis/ \ /home/nero/backups/configs/ \ --tag "daily,$(date +%A)" \ --exclude-caches \ --quiet
# Apply retention and prune in one step restic forget \ --keep-daily 7 \ --keep-weekly 4 \ --keep-monthly 6 \ --prune \ --quiet

antipattern.description: Backing up the entire server filesystem including .venv, node_modules, and Docker image layers.
antipattern.reason: Rebuilding venvs and pulling Docker images is faster than transferring gigabytes of them to B2. Oversized backups exhaust retention budgets faster and increase backup duration.

example.code:
# List all snapshots restic snapshots
# Restore latest snapshot to a directory restic restore latest --target /tmp/restore
# Restore specific file from latest restic restore latest --include "/home/nero/backups/database/nero_db.dump" --target /tmp
# Restore a specific snapshot by ID restic restore abc12345 --target /tmp/restore
# Mount repository as FUSE filesystem (browse files interactively) restic mount /mnt/restic-backup

section.name: wrapper-pattern

rule.statement: Create a restic-wrapper.sh that pre-loads all environment variables from .env so restic commands can be called without manually exporting variables.
rule.rationale: restic requires RESTIC_REPOSITORY, RESTIC_PASSWORD_FILE, and cloud credentials on every invocation. Without a wrapper, each ad-hoc restore command requires manually loading 4+ variables.

section.reference: restic-wrapper.sh: pre-loads env from .env, passes all args through to restic
